---
name: Frame Vocabulary Standard entry proposal
about: Propose a new named framing pattern for the FVS library
title: "[FVS proposal] "
labels: fvs-proposal
---

**Frame name**
A short noun phrase (e.g., "Growth Frame," "Completeness Illusion").
See `data/frame_library/INDEX.md` for the naming style existing
entries follow.

**One-paragraph definition**
What perspective does this frame take? What does it make visible?
What does it make invisible?

**Identification signals**
How does a reader recognize this frame in a document? Concrete
vocabulary, structural patterns, or detectable signatures. If a
computational rule is plausible, sketch it (e.g., "high trends
coverage AND missing risks"). Rule-formality is not required at
proposal stage; reviewers will work with the curator to align.

**Adjacent frames**
Which existing FVS entries are adjacent (related but distinct)?
Name at least one. Adjacency must be reciprocal, so the existing
entry will also need to reference back; this is part of the
promotion work.

**Worked example**
A specific document excerpt where this frame is visible. Citation
optional but helpful; the corpus team publishes worked examples
with full citation chains.

**Honest limits**
When is this frame NOT the right lens? Common false positives? See
existing entries (e.g., `data/frame_library/FVS-008_growth_frame.md`
§"Honest limits") for the pattern.

**Status request**
Tick one:

- [ ] `aspirational`: reserving a FVS ID; full entry to follow
- [ ] `draft`: full entry below is ready for review

**Reviewer context**
See `CONTRIBUTING.md §"Proposing a new FVS library entry"` for the
full mechanical process. See `GOVERNANCE.md §canon-promotion` for
the promotion criteria a frame must meet to move from `draft` to
`canon`. Zero frames are currently `canon`; first promotion will
go through the `REVIEWERS.md` pipeline.
